Al Gharafa are interested in Cesc Fabregas (AS Monaco)

Sevilla intensify talks to sign Marseille midfielder Maxime Lopez

Estadio Deportivo reports that Spanish side Sevilla have in recent days intensified discussions over the possible signing of 22-year-old French midfielder Maxime Lopez from Marseille.

Sporting Director Monchi is a big fan of the player, with whom the outlet claims he already has a provisional 5-year contract agreement sorted out with.

There is currently no fee agreement between the two clubs, but there has been renewed conversations over a deal since the departure of Sporting Director Andoni Zubizarreta for the player who is currently under contract with OM until June 2021.

Marseille are seeking at least €12m in order to part with a player who has just 11 months remaining on his current contract. Via intermediaries, it is understood that Sevilla are currently proposing €8m in order to conclude a deal.

In a few days therefore, a long-runner soap opera could finally decant, with Sevilla having had bids for Lopez rejected in both of the last two windows. This time, Monchi wants to get his man more than ever.

UPDATE: CHELSEA AND CHILWELL

Sky Sports News reporter Kaveh Solhekol has provided an update on Chelsea’s pursuit of Leicester left-back Ben Chilwell.

“In the background, talks are under way and plans are being made about Chelsea’s transfer strategy this summer,” he said. “What Frank Lampard really wants is a new goalkeeper, and another left-back.

“His number one target is Ben Chilwell. The problem is he is under long-term contract at Leicester, who are under no pressure to sell.

“It all depends on what Chilwell is going to do – is he going to tell Leicester he wants to leave, he wants to play in the Champions League, he wants to play for Chelsea?

“Even if he did that, Leicester could turn around and says, ‘sorry, you’re under contract, you’re going nowhere’.”

Sevilla Sporting Director Monchi interested in former Barcelona winger Malcom

According to Estadio Deportivo, Sevilla’s Sporting Director Monchi is interested in buying former Barcelona winger Malcom.

Monchi has been monitoring the player since he was at Brazilian club Corinthians. When Malcom made the move to Europe with French side Bordeaux, Monchi came close to signing him during his spell as Sporting Director at Roma. However, after Roma had announced an agreement with Bordeaux to sign Malcom, Barça managed to convince him to move to Camp Nou with a last-minute bid.

The 23-year-old made just 24 appearances for the Catalan club, before moving to Zenit Saint Petersburg.

RB Leipzig don’t want to pay €25m for Milot Rashica; agent in Milan

Bild report tonight that RB Leipzig don’t want to pay the €25 million transfer fee for 24-year-old Werder Bremen attacker Milot Rashica.

Speaking to Sky Germany, Werder sporting director Frank Baumann confirmed that Rashica has agreed personal terms with an unnamed club.

“It’s true that Milot can imagine a transfer, that there have been interested parties and that he has an agreement with a club he wants to join. The next weeks will show whether a transfer will take place,” said Baumann.

Altin Lala, Rashica’s agent, posted on Instagram that he’s on his way to Milan, presumably to speak to representatives of AC Milan, who have shown an interest in signing the Kosovo international.

Monaco strike €14m plus bonuses deal with Reims for Axel Disasi

L’Équipe report that AS Monaco have struck an agreement with Reims over the signing of 22-year-old French central defender Axel Disasi for €14m plus bonuses.

Negotiations have been ongoing between the two clubs since the middle of June and concluded on Monday with a €14m initial fee agreement, plus €3m in bonuses.

Sporting Director Paul Mitchell has set out his stall in seeking young players with considerable top flight experience.

Disasi had 12 months remaining on his Reims deal.

Niko Kovac looking at Kevin Vogt (Hoffenheim) & Marius Wolf (Dortmund)

France Football report that AS Monaco manager Niko Kovac is seeking to strengthen his side’s defence with a signing from the Bundesliga this summer.

He is a big fan of 28-year-old Kevin Vogt, who spent the last six months on loan at Werder Bremen from Hoffenheim. Kovac had wanted to sign the central defender at Bayern Munich, but could not convince the Bavarian board to go through with it.

He has also mentioned 25-year-old right-back Marius Wolf, to replace Benjamin Henrichs who has joined RB Leipzig on loan with an option to buy. Kovac managed the player at Frankfurt – BVB will want €10m in order to part with Wolf.

Inter will take the opportunity of their stay in Germany for the Europa League to have a meeting with Bayern bosses and discuss both the future of Ivan Perišić and a potential swap between Corentin Tolisso and Marcelo Brozović [Sportske Novosti]

Perišić wants to stay at Bayern as he intends to raise his kids in Germany. Inter want to sell the Croatian but Bayern have no intention to pay the asking price unless it's significantly lowered. The Premier League remains an option for Perišić, but his priority is Bayern.

The second topic of discussion could be Marcelo Brozović. The Croatian was wanted by Niko Kovač but Rummenigge vetoed the move back then due to the €60m pricetag. Inter now would negotiate a swap deal with Corentin Tolisso but are asking for €20m plus the Frenchman

Hertha Berlin would be willing to meet Weston McKennie demands; Monaco & Southampton interested

According to Sky Germany, Hertha Berlin lead the race to sign 21-year-old Schalke midfielder Weston McKennie.

The US international has attracted interest from Premier League club Southampton and Ligue 1 club AS Monaco. However, neither club want to meet Schalke’s €25 million asking price nor McKennie’s salary.

Per Sky, Southampton were surprised with the financial backing at Hertha and even with the imminent departure of Pierre-Emile Højbjerg, the Saints would not be willing to spend such a fee on a replacement.

French outlet L’Équipe reported yesterday that AS Monaco are in talks to sign McKennie after Niko Kovač was given the green light.

Fellow Bundesliga club Hertha would be willing to meet Schalke’s demands with the backing of investor Lars Windhorst.
